def: v.t. To sacrifice; to devote

(The nurse or mother's expostulation with her infant). qurbān k. , Mah. Wom. v. a. To offer up a sacrifice. 1. Tujhe apnī eṛī choṭī par se qurbān karūṅ! I 'll whirl you from my head to heel for a sacrifice. 2. Karūṅ qurbān maiṅ pishvāz ko jālī kī kurtī par! Ni-bakhtā! mujh se uṭh se uṭh saktā nahīṅ hai bojh dāman kā . Wom. Rangin. To sacrifice oneself for another. Yeh us par sau jān se qurbān haiṅ. Īd-i-qurbāṅ . A festival observed by Mahomadans on the 10th of zilhijja , when animals are sacrificed in commemoration of Abraham's attempted sacrifice of Ish…

s.f. (colloq.), corr. of kurīz, q.v. s.f. Moulting (of birds)
s.f. Misconduct, bad manners, improper course.
s.f. A lady; mistress, proprietress
subs: adj. & adv. Lady-like; — in the manner of a lady.
wety: From Sanskrit क्रिया (kriyā).

def: s.f. The state of a bird sitting at ease and in security and preening its wings with its beak; (hence) ease, peace, comfort, quiet, tranquillity, security, confidence of success
more: n. f. 1. A bird sitting at ease and preening his wings with his beak. 2. Ease; tranquillity; comfort kuryāl meṅ ānā, karyāl k. , Slang. v. n. To flap one's wings; rejoice. kuryāl meṅ gullā lagnā , v. n. To be marred (one's pleasures).

s. f. 1. The state of a bird sitting at ease and in security, trimming its wings with its beak; (hence) 2. Ease, security, hope, confidence of success. کريال مين غليلا لگنا kuryāl meṅ g̠ẖulelā lagnā, To be disappointed or fall into misfortune, in the moment of security, or when sure of success. Sajjād says:-- بيٿهہ اگرخوشي سي آکر چمن مين بلبل کريال مين غليلا ايسا لگي که اڙجاي Baiṭhe agar ḵẖụshī se ākar chaman meṅ bulbul; kuryāl meṅ g̠ẖulelā aisā lage ki uṛ jāʼe. If the nightingale perch with pleasure in the midst of the parterre, in the moment of his security a ball will hit him with such force as to dash him to pieces.
subs: (or gulla) lagnā (-ko), To be struck by (the pellet of) misfortune in the moment of security or assurance; to be disappointed, or to have one's pleasure marred, when sure of success.

def: s.f. Moulting (of birds)
more: n. f. Moulting (of birds). kurīz k. , v. a. To moult (birds).
s. Moulting (of birds). کريز کرنا kurīz karnā, To moult (birds).

ety: prob. fr. A. قرق qaraqu, 'he put, or placed; he laid, or imposed,' &c.
def: s.m. An embargo; the act of seizing, attacking; seizure; confiscation, sequestration, attachment (in this sense qurqī is the more com. form in Urdu); — enclosure; prohibition, hindrance, prevention of access; — adj. Confiscated, sequestered, attached, distrained; forfeited
more: adj. Forfeited; distrained; attached; sequestered. qurq-amīn, qurq-sazāval . An officer of the court employed to attach a property and realize the proceeds; a bailiff; sheriff's officer. qurq-pizīr . Contraband; liable to seizure. qurq k. , v. a. To distrain; sequester; attach; take in execution. qurq-nāmah, parvānah-i-qurqī . A writ of execution. qurq-huā , adj. Attached; sequestered. qurq-hone-vālā . About to be taken in execution.
s. m. 1. An embargo. 2. Confiscation, the act of seizing, attacking, seizure. 3. Enclosure, hinderance, prevention of access.
subs: s.m. An officer (of a law-court) employed to attach property in execution of a decree and to realize the proceeds: a sheriff's officer, a bailiff
v.t. To seize or take in execution, to attach (property), to distrain, to sequester
s.m. A writ of execution ( = qurqī-parwāna)
v.n. To be seized, be attached, &c.

def: adj. Confiscated, attached, &c.; — s.f. Confiscation, sequestration, attachment, distraint ( = qurq)
more: n. f. Distraint; distress; attachment; sequestration. qurqī uṭhā lenā, yā bar-khuāst k., qurqī se chhuṛānā; vā-guzasht, yā khalās k. , v. a. To withdraw or remove an attachment or distraint. qurqī biṭhānā , v. a. 1. To set a guard over distrained property. 2. To prevent egress from a house. qurqī bhejnā , v. a. To send an officer to execute an attachment. qurqī-i-ām . A general attachment. qurqī qabal faislah . An attachment before judgment. qurqī kā parvānā . v. a. A warrant of attachment. qurqī karne-vālā, āmil-i-qurqī , n. m. The officer executing a distress; distrainer. kharcha-i-qurqī . Costs of distress. māl kāfī qābil-i-qurqī . ( Law ) Sufficient distress.
adj. Confiscated. قرقي پروانه ḳurḳ-ī parwāna, A warrant issued to sequester a property.
subs: (-se), To withdraw or remove an attachment or distraint
(with loc.), To set a guard over distrained or attached property; to prevent egress from (a house, &c.)
To send an officer to execute an attachment
or qurqī-parwāna, or parwāna-ě-qurqī, s.m. A warrant issued to sequester a property, a warrant of attachment, writ of execution ( = qurq-nāma).

def: n. m. A whole unripe pea.

ety: fr. S. कुर्, by redupl.; cf. kurkurāna
def: v.n. To rumble (as the bowels); to grumble, murmur, growl, speak angrily; to be sullen, to fume; to be grudging or niggardly; to cackle, to cluck as a hen that wants to sit, or as a laying hen).
more: 1. To cluck, to cackle (a laying hen). 2. To speak angrily, to murmur, to grumble. ¶ kiṛkiṛānā, To gnash the teeth from rage. ¶ kaṛkaṛānā, To give such a sound as oil or butter when boiling.

def: s.f. A rumbling in the bowels, borborygm; the gripes, a dangerous complaint to which horses are subject: — kurkurī uṭhnā; v.n. To have a rumbling in the bowels; to have the gripes; (met.) evil to come upon (one).
more: n. f. Gripes; borborygm. kurkurī, kurkurā , adj. Crispy; brittle. kurkurī uṭhnā , v. n. 1. To have the gripes or colic. 2. See shāmat ānā. kurkurī haḍḍī , n. f. Brittle bone.
s. (dakh.) A dangerous bowel complaint to which horses are subject.

def: s.f. Rubbish, sweepings.
wety: Borrowed from English cricket.

def: s.m. A tortoise, a turtle; the second avatār or incarnation of Vishṇu (his descent in the form of a tortoise to support the mountain Mandara at the churning of the ocean)
more: s. m. 1. A tortoise, a turtle. 2. The name of the second Hindu incarnation: see under اوتار and see کچهپ. ¶ karm or karam (for कर्म्म ) 1. Action, act, business, office, fortune, fate, destiny. کرم بهوگ karam-bhog, s. m. Fulfilling of destiny. 2. Accusative case. 3. Hindū worship, devotion. 4. Name of a bird.
subs: s.m. The fifteenth Purāṇa, which contains the history of the tortoise-incarnation of Vishṇu.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَرَم (karam), from Arabic كَرَم.

def: s.m. A tortoise, a turtle ( = kurm, q.v.). s.m. A tortoise, a turtle; the second avatār or incarnation of Vishṇu (his descent in the form of a tortoise to support the mountain Mandara at the churning of the ocean)
subs: s.m. The fifteenth Purāṇa, which contains the history of the tortoise-incarnation of Vishṇu.

def: (dakh.) To cranch ( Harris ).
s.m. Family, kindred, dependant, follower; race, tribe, caste
more: s. m. Tribe, cast, family, race. کڙمچودي kuṛum-chodī, Incest.
subs: s.f. Incest (kuṛum is a contract. of kuṛma).

def: s.m. A caste of agriculturists in eastern and central Hindūstān (being the same essentially as the kumbī of the west and south); a man of that caste.
more: s. m. A tribe of husbandmen, called also kunbī.

def: s.m. The white-crested Kalij pheasant (of the sub-Himālayas), Gallophasis albocristatus.
more: The white-crested Kalij pheasant; Gallophasis albocristatus . A resident of the lower and sub-Himalāyās.

def: s.f. Murmuring, grumbling; rumbling (of the bowels)
more: n. m. A grating or crackling sound (a dog gnawing a bone).

def: v.n. (dialec.) = kuṛhnā, q.v. v.n. To be vexed or annoyed; to be disgusted; to be afflicted; to be grieved or distressed (for); — to grieve, mourn, lament, repine, pine, fret.

ety: = kornī, fem. of kornā = koṛnā, q.v.
def: s.f. A scoop (syn. karćhī); a carving or engraving tool, &c.
v.t. (dialec.) To scrape, &c. ( = khuraćnā, q.v.). v.t. To scrape; to scratch; to scratch out, to erase
s.m. A colt, foal (one or two years old, of a horse, or camel, or ass).
adj. Extremely or most generous, very beneficent, most noble.

def: s.f. Cheerfulness, brightness, joy, gladness

def: Coolness of eye, viz. cheerfulness, tranquillity.
s.f. Gristle, cartilage.
wety: Borrowed from the nominative singular of Sanskrit करिन् (karin), from कर (kara) + -इन् (-in). कर (kara) is from the root कृ (kṛ), while the suffix -इन् (-in) is from Proto-Indo-European *-iHnos. Related to کَر (kar, “hand, elephant's trunk”).

ety: v.n. fr. قرص 'to take with the finger and thumb'; &c.
def: s.m. A ball of leaven, or paste,' &c.; a pellet; an orb; disc (of the sun or moon); a round cake (of bread; or of soap, &c.); a biscuit; a wafer; a pastil; a lozenge; any medicine made up as a lozenge or wafer; — a small silver coin (worth about twopence).
more: n. m. 1. The disc of the sun. 2. Any thing in the shape of a wafer, lozenge, or biscuit; a cake of soap. 3. ( Med .) Any medicine made up as a wafer. 4. A silver coin worth about two pence.
s. m. An orb, a pellet, a ball of paste, the disk (of the sun or moon).
s. m. Bread of a round form.

def: (dakh.) An ornament worn by women round the waist: see کمرسال kamar-sāl below ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.m. A kind of coarse sugar.
more: s. m. A kind of coarse sugar.

def: s.f. A chair, seat, stool; chair of state, throne; bench (of a judge); the eighth (or, according to some, the ninth) heaven, the crystalline or the empyrean heaven (as being the supposed throne of God); the base of a pillar; a pedestal; a raised foundation, a plinth; — genealogy; — name of a place within about fourteen miles of Lakhnau (Lucknow), the inhabitants of which are said to be foolish or mad
more: n. f. 1. A. chair; seat. 2. A throne. 3. The base of a pillar. 4. A raised foundation; plinth. 5. The eighth heaven, or, according to some, the ninth; the throne of God. 6. Genealogy. 7. A village about Lucknow, proverbial for the stupidity of its inhabitants. kursī denā , v. a. 1. To give plinth or elevation to. 2. To give one a chair. Use to Lāṭh sāhab kursī dete haiṅ. kursī kā uhmaq . n. m. A perfect fool. kursī-nāmah, shajrah-i-nasb , n. A genealogical table or tree; genealogy. kursī-nashīṅ , adj. 1. Fixed; settled down. 2. Established in office; enthroned. 3. Verified. kursī nashin , n. m. One entitled to a seat in a Darbar, or to the honor of a chair. kursī nashīṅ h. , v. n. 1. To settle down. 2. To be admitted or accepted.

s. f. 1. A chair, a stool, a seat, a throne. 2. The eighth heaven, or according to some the ninth, the throne of God. 3. The name of a place, within about fourteen miles of Lakhnau, the inhabitants of which are said to be foolish or mad. کرسي کاهي kursī kā hai, He is from Kursī, i. e. he is mad or foolish. کرسي نامه kursī-nāma, A genealogical tree. کرسي نشين kursī-nishīn, Established in office, enthroned; verified.
s. (dakh.) The base or foot of a pillar ( Harris ).
subs: (-ko), To give a chair (to); to give plinth or elevation (to)
'He is from Kursī,' he is a stupid fellow, or a fool
s.m. A genealogical table or tree

adj. & s.m. Seated in a chair, enthroned; established in office; fixed, settled down; settled in the mind, verified; — one who is entitled to a chair or seat in a Darbār, one who is entitled to the honour of a chair.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُرْسِی (kursī), borrowed from Arabic كُرْسِيّ (kursiyy), borrowed from Aramaic כּוּרְסְיָא (kursəyā) or Classical Syriac ܟܘܪܣܝܐ (kursəyā).

def: s.m. Cotton, &c. put into an inkstand; a cloth used by menstruous women; a pessary.
more: s. m. 1. Cotton, &c. put into an ink-stand. 2. A cloth used by menstruous women. 3. A pessary.

s. m. A frock, a kind of shirt, reaching down to the hips (or ankles) with very short if any sleeves, sometimes open at the upper part of the chest in front (see Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: kuṛtā, s. m. (dakh.) See کرته kurta.

more: Māṛ. n. f. S. कुलत्य A kind of vetch, or small thin white bean, used as food for horses; Dolichos biflorus , Wat. Buṛbak debī ke kurthī ke achchhat . Prov. Any thing will do for an inferior goddess.
s. f. A kind of vetch. (Dolichos biflorus).

def: s.f. A bodice; a chemisette; — a (soldier's) jacket; a coat.
more: n. f. 1. A woman's boddice or chemisette. 2. A soldier's jacket; a coat. Jāt pāt pūchhe nā koë, kurtī paihn tilaṅgā hoë . Prov. Nor caste nor status asked or known; a coat put on, a soldier grown.
(from کرته) s. f. A waistcoat or shirt for women, a jacket for soldiers, a coat.

def: s. (dakh. i. q. جانورکوپاني پلاني کاظرف) A vessel for giving water in to an animal.

s.m. Name of an ancient sovereign of Dehli and the surrounding country; name of the territory governed by him; and of the people of that country; name of the most northerly of the four Mahādvīps or principal divisions of the known world (called also uttar kuru; by other systems, it is considered one of the nine varshas or divisions of the known world; in each case, however, it is the country beyond the northernmost range of mountains extending to the frozen ocean)
more: n. prop. Name of an ancient sovereign of Dehli and the country around.
subs: vulg. kuru-ćhetr, s.m. 'The field of the Kurus,' name of a region or extensive plain near Dehli, the scene of the great battles between the Kauravas and the Pāṇḍavas; — the inhabitants of that country
vulg. kuru-baṅs, s.m. The race of Kuru
vulg. kuru-baṅsī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Belonging to the race of Kuru; a man of that race.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कुरु (kuru).

def: s.m. corr. of kuru-kshetr, q.v.s.v. کرو kuru.
more: n. prop. The country near Dihlī, where the great battle was fought between the Kurus and Pānḍus.

def: s.f. Depraved appetite, low taste; disinclination, distaste, dislike, aversion, disgust; weariness, languor, lassitude.
s.f. (dialec.) A coil (of string or rope); a thread or wire (of metal).
s.m. A curl, lock (of hair).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کُس (kus, “pussy, cunt”).

def: s.f. = کشل kuśal, q.v.
more: s. f. Welfare, wellbeing, health, prosperity, safety.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Right, proper, suitable, good; well, healthy, in good condition; happy, prosperous; auspicious; competent, able, skilful, expert, clever; — s.f. & m. Health, well-being, welfare, safety, prosperity, happiness
more: n. f. S. कोशल welfare; Pr. kosalo, kausalo . See /?/khair . 1. An-mile kī kusal hai . Prov. Saying. You are safe till you meet with your match. 2. Yahāṅ khem kushal hai, tumhārī khem kushal sadā bhalī chāhiye . (A letter). We are all well here, hoping you are the same.

vulg. kusal-ćhem, s.f. & m. Health and well-being

more: n. m. Evil company. Basi ku-saṅg chāhat kushal? Tulsī! ye man soch! Mahimā ghaṭī samudr kī Rāvan baso paṛ/?/s . Tulsī Dās. Tulsī! just think! who can good get in evil company? Through Ravan's neighbourhood declined the greatness of the sea. (The water became saltish).

s.f. = kuśalāt = kuśal, q.v.

ety: rt. of kushtan; Zend kush = S. कुष्
def: part. & s.m. Killing, destroying; subduing; killer, &c. (used as last member of comp., e.g. dev-kush, 'demon-destroyer, giant-killer').
more: s. m. Name of a grass, the sacred grass of the Hindūs. (Poa cynosuroides).

def: s.m. An aviary.

ety: rt. of kushādan = gushādan = gushūdan; rt. Zend shu with vi = S. वि+च्यु
def: part. Opening, expanding; displaying; loosening; solving; revealing; — conquering (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-kushā, 'heart-expanding'; — mushkil-kushā, 'difficulty-solving').
more: part. act. of کشادن to draw. ( In Compos .) 1. Opening; expanding. Dil-kushā . Heart-expanding. 2 Solving. Mushkil-kushā . Difficulty-solving.

n. m. S. कुश Pālī, kuso . A grass; poa cynosuroides ; sacred grass. It is esteemed holy from its being strewed upon the alter or held by the Brahmins in their hands whilst repeating their formulæ. It is also given at bathing places to pilgrims. kushā-āsan , n. A mat made of kushā grass.
part. act. (of کشادن) in compos. 1. Opening, displaying, expanding, solving, loosening. 2. Conquering.

def: s.f. Opening, loosening, untying; expansion; cheerfulness (cf. kushādagī); — part. adj. contrac. of kushāda, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of kushādan; see kushā
def: part. adj. Opened, uncovered, disclosed, discovered, detected, revealed, expanded, spread out, displayed, drawn forth; taken, subjected, subdued; open, spacious, wide, ample, capacious, extensive; loose, lax; free, frank, cheerful, glad, happy; serene, clear
more: See فراخ 1. Open. 2. Spacious. kushādah-abrū , adj. Open or wide between the eyebrows. kushādah-dil , adj. Openhearted; frank. kushādah-ravī , n. f. Straddling (of a horse, etc.) in his gait. kushādah kushādah , adv. 1. A part; aloof; separately; asunder. 2. Distinctly; plainly; clearly. Kushādah kushādah likkho!

(contract. کشاد kushād ) part. pass. (of کشادن) Open, uncovered, expanded, wide, ample, extensive, spacious, capacious, loose, lax, displayed, revealed, free, cheerful, frank, drawn forth. کشاده ابرو kushāda-abru, adj. Open or wide between the eyebrows. کشاده پيشاني kushāda-peshānī, or کشاده جبين kushāda-jabīn, or کشاده روي kushāda-rūʼe, adj. Of an open countenance, gay, cheerful, blithe. کشاده دل kushāda-dil, adj. Openhearted, frank. کشاده رو kushāda-rau, Epithet of a horse that walks wide or straddles behind. کشاده روي kushāda-rūʼī, s. f. Openness of countenance, cheerfulness. ¶ kushāda-rau-ī, s. f. Straddling (of a horse, &c.) in gait.
subs: adj. Open or wide between the eyebrows; of open countenance
or kushāda-jabīn, adj. Having a broad forehead, of an open countenance; gay, cheerful, blithe
s.f. Openness of heart, cheerfulness, hilarity; liberality, generosity
s.f. Straddling (of a horse, &c.) in gait
adj. Of open, serene countenance; gay, &c. ( = kushāda-peshānī)
s.f. Openness of countenance, cheerfulness, brightness of face
adv. Wide apart, apart, aloof, asunder, separately; clearly, distinctly, plainly.

def: s.f. An opening, an aperture; openness, expansion, spaciousness, extension, extensiveness; looseness; latitude; openness of heart, joy, cheerfulness, exhilaration.
more: n. f. 1. Expansion; extension. 2. Openness; spaciousness.
